title: Massive fuel price decrease on the cards in July
bodyText:
GARDEN ROUTE | KAROO NEWS - Early predictions of a massive decrease in fuel prices may become a reality at the pumps in July, providing much-needed relief for motorists.

Recent data from the Central Energy Fund indicates that fuel recoveries have been in favour of drivers, with both petrol and diesel showing substantial over-recoveries this month.

If these trends continue, July will mark the second consecutive month of fuel price cuts.

In June, fuel prices decreased by as much as R1.24 per litre, while diesel prices dropped by between R1.08 and R1.18 per litre.


> How to save fuel:


- Keep tyres at the right pressure: Underinflated tyres have a higher rolling resistance, generating more friction, which increases fuel consumption
- Use the aircon: Driving with the windows down at speeds faster than 80km/h causes wind resistance and more fuel is used. It’s more fuel-efficient to drive with the aircon on
- Plan your rush-hour route: Stop-start traffic burns more fuel. When possible, plan your commute to avoid peak-hour traffic
- Don’t stay idle for long: Turn your engine off if you idle for longer than three minutes. Idling burns fuel.

Read original story on www.citizen.co.za

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
